subject: [DCAEGEN2] Deploy Datalake components(feeder, des, admin-ui) via Helm
commit_message:
[DCAEGEN2] Deploy Datalake components(feeder, des, admin-ui) via Helm

    DCAE Helm Transformation (Phase-2).

Signed-off-by: Niranjana <niranjana.y60@wipro.com>

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-2

I haven't got answer to my previous question

Am I missing sth or you are exposing new http NodePorts with this change?
If yes then what's the SECCOM opinion on this?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review-2
> 
> I haven't got answer to my previous question
> 
> Am I missing sth or you are exposing new http NodePorts with this change?
> If yes then what's the SECCOM opinion on this?

Hi Krzysztof, Maybe I misunderstood, I though you wanted me to add 'plain_port' to mandate the use of http(according to the documentation). I am able to access the service inside the kubernetes cluster using  port number 1680, and outside the cluster using node port 30408.
Here is the blueprint which I had referred for helm transformation.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/platform/blueprints/+/120607

For the second question yes it is a NodePort as mentioned in the blueprints.
SECCOM opinion for exposing NodePort? @Vijay or @Krzysztof could you please explain? I wasn't aware I had check SECCOM for this since it was already a NodePort in the cloudify blueprint.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 5: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > I haven't got answer to my previous question
> > 
> > Am I missing sth or you are exposing new http NodePorts with this change?
> > If yes then what's the SECCOM opinion on this?
> 
> Hi Krzysztof, Maybe I misunderstood, I though you wanted me to add 'plain_port' to mandate the use of http(according to the documentation). I am able to access the service inside the kubernetes cluster using  port number 1680, and outside the cluster using node port 30408.
> Here is the blueprint which I had referred for helm transformation.
> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/platform/blueprints/+/120607
> 
> For the second question yes it is a NodePort as mentioned in the blueprints.
> SECCOM opinion for exposing NodePort? @Vijay or @Krzysztof could you please explain? I wasn't aware I had check SECCOM for this since it was already a NodePort in the cloudify blueprint.


Niranjana - You are right, the DL components after introduction under POC in earlier release (Frankfurt) was not maintained hence http port addressal was overlooked.

For current helm migration charts, would suggest to replace the service definition from nodeport to clusterIP. As there is no other component accessing Feeder or DES externally, these module doesn't require nodeport exposure. The AdminUI though will require access outside cluster (for UI access) hence this should be handled for https. I will create separate jira to update the nginx based webserver needs to be switched to https. For this patch, we can switch AdminUI configuration also to ClusterIP and document instruction to setup port-forwarding or nodeport when UI access is required.

@Krzysztof - As chart default do not expose any HTTP connection via Nodeport, I Hope this is acceptable solution to move forward with this patch.
